text: Extracorporeal shock wave therapy is the only plantar fasciitis treatment with evidence of medium and long-term pain reduction compared to control.
Low-dye taping provides the largest short-term pain reduction among plantar fasciitis treatments evaluated in randomized controlled trials.
Platelet-rich plasma injection is effective for short-term pain relief in plantar fasciitis.
Stretching is effective for short-term plantar fasciitis pain relief, though with a smaller effect size than other interventions.
Dry needling is effective for short-term pain reduction in plantar fasciitis.
Low-level laser therapy is effective for short-term plantar fasciitis pain relief with a narrow confidence interval suggesting consistent results.
Most therapeutic interventions for plantar fasciitis only demonstrate short-term pain relief, with effects not sustained in medium or long-term follow-up.
